*�����������������QAttribute POP creates attributes on Points, Vertices and Primitives - They are assigned a constant value that you  specify.

Attributes include a built-in set (Color, N, Text, PointScale ...)
...
or are user-defined.

Attribute POP lets you duplicate, rename, delete attributes.

and you can create arrays of Attributes.  DaysPerMonth[12] may be an attribute made of 12 integers.

Many operators, like Math POP can easily create attributes by simply giving a name in the Output Attribute Scope parameter.

The operator will figure out what type and size it should be, but you can override it.
